title: Python Basics
subtitle: Build your first Python instincts, one tiny mission at a time.

questions.prompt: Which symbol starts a comment in Python?
questions.explanation: Python ignores everything after a # on the same line, so it is used for comments.
questions.commonMistake: Many beginners use // because they have seen it in JavaScript or Java, but Python comments start with #.

questions.explanation: The print function sends text or values to the screen.

questions.hint: Look for the option wrapped in quote marks. Quotes usually signal text in Python.
questions.explanation: Text wrapped in quotes is a string. Both single and double quotes work in Python.
Multiplication happens before addition, so 3 * 4 is 12, then 2 + 12 is 14.
questions.commonMistake: A common mistake is adding 2 + 3 first. Python follows operator precedence, so multiplication runs before addition.

questions.prompt: Which line creates a variable named score?
questions.hint: Python assignment usually reads like name equals value, without let or var.
questions.explanation: Python creates variables with the assignment operator: name = value. The variable name goes on the left, and the value being stored goes on the right.

questions.prompt: Which type is used for True or False values?
questions.explanation: Boolean values use the bool type. The two boolean literals are True and False.
len returns the number of items in a collection. This list has three items.

questions.prompt: Which keyword starts a conditional branch?
questions.explanation: Python uses if to run code only when a condition is true.

questions.prompt: Which loop is best for visiting every item in a list?
questions.hint: For a list, think about the loop form that names one item at a time.
questions.explanation: A for loop can visit each item in a list using syntax like for item in items:.
input reads what the user types and returns it as a string.
